Roles & Permissions

Merlin uses the Spatie Laravel Permission package to control who can do what. Every action in the application is gated by a named permission. Roles are collections of permissions that can be freely created and renamed through the UI; permissions are the stable code contract that never changes without a migration.

How authorization works

All 16 policies in app/Policies/ extend AllModulesPolicy. Authorization checks are performed with $user->can('permission-name') — never with $user->hasRole(). This is a hard rule: roles are user-configurable at runtime, so a check like hasRole('Administrator') would break the moment an administrator renames their role. Permissions are the stable code contract.

The Administrator role

The super-admin role is named Administrator (not "Super Admin" or "Admin"). It is seeded by the RolesAndPermissionsSeeder and is given all permissions by the permission migrations. A second pre-seeded role, accountant, is also created by the permissions migrations and is given a default set of operational permissions (access-panel, invoice workflow, posting rules, reprocessing). Both role names can be used as the basis for custom roles, but the names themselves are not special to the application code — only the permissions attached to them matter.

Permission naming

Permissions follow two naming shapes:

  • Model permissions — {resource}-{action}, where resource is a kebab-case model name and action is one of view-any, view, create, update, delete, restore, force-delete
  • Workflow permissions — can-{action} or a short descriptor for cross-cutting capabilities (e.g. access-panel, view-llm-summary)

Model permissions

The following resources each have a full set of CRUD permissions:

ResourcePermission prefix
Accountsaccounts-
Account groupsaccount-groups-
Account typesaccount-types-
Addressesaddresses-
Businessesbusinesses-
Contact assignmentscontact-assignments-
Documentsdocuments-
Document activitiesdocument-activities-
Document linesdocument-lines-
Document relationshipsdocument-relationships-
LLM logsllm-logs-
Partiesparties-
Party relationshipsparty-relationships-
Personspersons-
Usersusers-
Posting rulesposting-rules-
Payment termspayment-terms-
Recurring invoicesrecurring-invoices-

Each resource supports the actions: view-any, view, create, update, delete, restore, force-delete.

Workflow permissions

These permissions gate specific actions that cut across models or represent sensitive operations:

PermissionWhat it controls
access-panelEntry to the admin UI; users without this permission cannot reach any page after login
view-llm-summaryViewing the AI confidence summary and detailed extraction data on invoices and in reports
can-review-invoicesMarking a purchase invoice as reviewed
can-authorise-invoicesApproving a purchase invoice for posting
can-post-invoicesPosting a purchase invoice to the general ledger (terminal action)
can-reprocess-invoicesDiscarding AI extraction results and re-running the full pipeline on an invoice
can-process-invoice-paymentsProcessing payment transactions against purchase invoices
can-record-paymentsRecording payments received against sales invoices
can-send-sales-invoicesEmailing a sales invoice to the client
can-void-sales-invoicesVoiding a sales invoice (sets status to voided; terminal action)

Managing roles in the UI

Go to Settings › Roles (/roles) to create new roles, rename existing roles, and attach or remove permissions from any role. You need the appropriate permission to access this page. Roles created here are immediately available for assignment to users.

Assigning permissions to users

Permissions are assigned to users indirectly through roles. Go to Settings › Users (/users) to edit a user and select their role. A user can hold one role at a time. The permissions attached to that role determine what the user can do across the entire application. See Users.
